mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of triggered months, or the signal fails to separate winning from losing periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates during early recovery phases, signaling capacity utilization improvements that boost corporate earnings expectations and generate positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during recovery phases, or forward returns show no statistical difference from baseline in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Payroll increases coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in >58% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 3.0 percent forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy longer, increasing recession probabilities and depressing quarterly equity valuations.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation periods, or the threshold fails to predict return direction with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising GDP growth and falling real personal consumption indicates supply-side bottlenecks rather than demand weakness, prompting equity market rallies on earnings resilience.
failure_condition: Divergence periods produce negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in industrial production during late-cycle peaks signal imminent manufacturing contraction, triggering rapid portfolio de-risking and negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or signal accuracy drops below 50% in this regime.
